title: PCI-DSS 12.10 — Incident response: IR readiness — playbook exercise evidence
exclusions: Does NOT run the exercise; measures whether the CDE IR playbook has been exercised within policy cadence (annually) and if the exercise produced findings.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_12_10_ir_exercise' (weekly) emits per playbook: playbook_id, scope_cde, last_exercise_at, finding_count, exercise_lead, cadence_met, to compliance_summary.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A CDE playbook with no drill event in 13 months returns cadence_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A playbook exercised within the year is absent.

spl:
| inputlookup ir_playbook_register.csv
| where scope_cde="yes"
| join type=outer playbook_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=ir:drill earliest=-14mon
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_exercise_at latest(finding_count) AS finding_count latest(exercise_lead) AS exercise_lead BY playbook_id]
| eval cadence_met=if(last_exercise_at>=now()-365*86400,1,0)
| table playbook_id scope_cde last_exercise_at finding_count exercise_lead cadence_met
description: PCI-DSS 12.10.2 requires IR plan testing at least annually. This UC evidences the test happened per CDE playbook.
value: Converts the 'we did a tabletop' email into indexed evidence auditors can query directly.
implementation: (1) IR exercise tool writes drill events to index=testing; (2) maintain ir_playbook_register.csv; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) cadence_met=0 schedules a drill; (5) annual Board report. Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ir:drill`, `sourcetype=ir:drill;`.
visualization: Table of playbooks with drill history, bar chart of findings per exercise, single value 'playbooks overdue'. Key fields: `cadence_met`, `exercise_lead`, `finding_count`, `last_exercise_at`, `playbook_id`, `playbook_id]`.
knownFalsePositives: Combined multi-playbook drills may link to one of several playbook_ids — maintain a drill_to_playbook mapping.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for pci-dss 12.10.2 requires ir plan testing at least annually. this uc evidences the test happened per cde playbook. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=testing` sourcetype=ir:drill; playbook register `ir_playbook_register.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) IR exercise tool writes drill events to index=testing; (2) maintain ir_playbook_register.csv; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) cadence_met=0 schedules a drill; (5) annual Board report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup ir_playbook_register.csv
| where scope_cde="yes"
| join type=outer playbook_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=ir:drill earliest=-14mon
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_exercise_at latest(finding_count) AS finding_count latest(exercise_lead) AS exercise_lead BY playbook_id]
| eval cadence_met=if(last_exercise_at>=now()-365*86400,1,0)
| table playbook_id scope_cde last_exercise_at finding_count exercise_lead cadence_met
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 12.10 — Incident response: IR readiness — playbook exercise evidence** — Converts the 'we did a tabletop' email into indexed evidence auditors can query directly.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=testing` sourcetype=ir:drill; playbook register `ir_playbook_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Filters the current rows with `where scope_cde="yes"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cadence_met** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: cadence_met, exercise_lead, finding_count, last_exercise_at, playbook_id, playbook_id], scope_cde.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of playbooks with drill history, bar chart of findings per exercise, single value 'playbooks overdue'.
